role_title: Product Manager, Structured Investments - Vice President / Senior Vice President
secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with people management and platform leadership

level_signals: Experience managing and developing product managers
VP / Senior Vice President title range

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 9+ years of product management experience, preferably within fintech, wealth management, capital markets, or related industries
Demonstrated success owning and scaling complex product domains in fast-paced, high-growth, or highly ambiguous environments
Proven ability to independently drive strategy, prioritization, and execution across large cross-functional organizations
Experience managing and developing product managers, including delegation, prioritization, and organizational leadership
Strong systems thinking and product judgment, with the ability to balance strategic vision, operational realities, and business impact
Willingness and initiative to become a deep expert in the structured investments space, understanding both the product intricacies and broader industry dynamics
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, including experience influencing senior business and engineering leaders
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a track record of navigating complex and unfamiliar challenges effectively
Technical acumen and comfortable partnering closely with engineering organizations to drive scalable platform solutions
Experience with agile methodologies and tools such as JIRA, Confluence, Figma, analytics dashboards, Postman, and modern AI productivity tools
Comfortable operating with a high degree of ownership, autonomy, and accountability
Able to balance strategic leadership with pragmatic execution in rapidly evolving environments

scope.surface: Structured investments platform — end-to-end product domains covering strategy, execution, and operational scaling
scope.business_model: B2B fintech platform serving wealth management firms, financial advisors, structured investment issuers, and wholesalers
primary_metrics_inferred: Business impact of product domains owned
core_responsibilities: Engage with wealth management firms, financial advisors, structured investments issuers, wholesalers, and executive stakeholders to identify strategic opportunities and shape the future direction of the structured investments platform
Own and lead major product domains and strategic initiatives across the platform, ensuring strong execution, scalability, and alignment with long-term business objectives
Drive product strategy, prioritization, roadmap planning, and cross-functional alignment across engineering, operations, sales, marketing, and other product organizations
Lead the end-to-end lifecycle of product development from conception through launch, iteration, and operational scaling
Mentor and develop product managers while fostering a culture of ownership, accountability, excellence, and continuous learning
Partner closely with engineering leadership to ensure strong execution, operational continuity, and effective prioritization across multiple concurrent workstreams
Communicate effectively with senior stakeholders and executive leadership regarding product strategy, prioritization, execution risks, and business impact
Leverage AI-powered tools and workflows to improve productivity, analysis, communication quality, and organizational effectiveness
Monitor the performance of product areas post-launch and continuously seek opportunities to improve user experience, scalability, and operational efficiency

green_flags: Equity included for all full-time employees
Comprehensive benefits package including 100% employer paid dental, vision, telemedicine, and virtual mental health counseling
Highly visible leadership role within a strategically important business unit
Hybrid schedule with Friday remote flexibility
red_flags: No specific city listed for the role despite a mandatory in-office Mon-Thu requirement — candidates must verify location
Wide title range (VP to SVP) with corresponding wide comp range ($160K-$230K) suggests significant ambiguity in actual seniority and compensation outcome
Fintech/wealth management domain experience listed as 'preferable' in language but treated as effectively required given structured investments context

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title range VP/SVP, years requirement, comp range, and people management scope)
scope.business_model (inferred from product and customer descriptions)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (no city specified in JD body; left empty)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from Mon-Thu in-office requirement; Friday remote only)
ambiguities: Industry experience in fintech/wealth management/capital markets is listed as 'preferably' in the qualifications, but the core role context (structured investments platform) makes domain familiarity effectively required. Classified as hard given overall JD context.
No city or office location is mentioned in the JD body; the in-office requirement is stated but location is unknown — candidates should confirm with recruiter.
Title range spans VP to SVP with a $70K base salary spread, creating ambiguity about how the level will be determined and what the final offer will look like.
